# UC-22.41.4 · TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike

## Description

Monitors observed TLS handshake metadata for any endpoint and alerts when legacy-cipher or legacy-protocol negotiations exceed a small threshold. Downgrade is a common attacker technique against partially-upgraded infrastructure.

## Value

Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure LBs emit handshake telemetry; (2) schedule hourly; (3) route hits to security ops; (4) block via config-as-code rollback. Indexes required: `index=tls`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=f5:tls`, `sourcetype=haproxy:tls`, `sourcetype=nginx:tls`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure LBs emit handshake telemetry; (2) schedule hourly; (3) route hits to security ops; (4) block via config-as-code rollback.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=tls sourcetype=f5:tls OR sourcetype=nginx:tls OR sourcetype=haproxy:tls earliest=-1h
| eval legacy=if(match(protocol,"TLSv1\.(0|1)") OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT"),1,0)
| stats sum(legacy) as legacy_hs count as total_hs by host
| eval legacy_pct=round(legacy_hs/total_hs*100,2)
| where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100
| table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike** — Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident.

Documented **Data sources**: F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: tls; **sourcetype**: f5:tls, nginx:tls, haproxy:tls. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=tls, sourcetype=f5:tls, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **legacy** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **host**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **legacy_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: host, legacy, legacy_hs, legacy_pct, total_hs.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the cryptographic inventory or PKI attestation runbook so encryption coverage matches what you claim in assessments.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart of legacy-handshake percentage per endpoint, table of offending endpoints, single-value '% handshakes on strong TLS (24h)'.

## SPL

```spl
index=tls sourcetype=f5:tls OR sourcetype=nginx:tls OR sourcetype=haproxy:tls earliest=-1h
| eval legacy=if(match(protocol,"TLSv1\.(0|1)") OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT"),1,0)
| stats sum(legacy) as legacy_hs count as total_hs by host
| eval legacy_pct=round(legacy_hs/total_hs*100,2)
| where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100
| table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct
```

## Visualization

Line chart of legacy-handshake percentage per endpoint, table of offending endpoints, single-value '% handshakes on strong TLS (24h)'.
